step.title: Write Sections (Iterate)
step.goal: Walk through every section in writing_order. For each: draft the section per its scope note, the Design Summary, and AGENT-BRIEF principles. Apply the section-quality checklist. Pause at planned checkpoints for user review.

substep.title: Draft This Section
substep.action:
Identify the current section: {{current_section}}. Pull its scope note from the Structure Outline. Pull any findings for this section from {{section_findings}}.
Draft the section body. Apply discipline from references/agent-brief-principles.md: - Durability over precision (no file paths in section body unless this is Risk Areas where evidence cites are bound) - Behavioral, not procedural (WHAT, not HOW) - Complete acceptance criteria where applicable (every AC independently testable) - Explicit scope boundaries where applicable - Reference linked DRs as wiki-links when the section content relates to a DR's decision - Cross-reference prior bundle artifacts (research file findings, Q&A entries) when content draws from them
Section structure: - Level-2 heading: "## {{current_section.name}}" - Body content per AGENT-BRIEF principles - If section has subsections (per scope note), use level-3 headings
Length target: aim for the estimate from the Writing Plan, but treat it as a target not a hard limit. 50% under or 50% over surfaces a quality concern (ticket-verify will catch).

substep.title: Apply Section-Quality Checklist
substep.action:
Run the section-quality checklist from agent-brief-principles.md against the drafted content:
- [ ] Content within the section's stated scope? - [ ] Durable identifiers, not file paths (Risk exception aside)? - [ ] Behavior, not implementation? - [ ] Testable claims actually testable as written? - [ ] Wiki-links work (DR aliases resolve)? - [ ] Consistent with Design Summary's promises?
If any answer is NO, re-draft this section before moving on. Do NOT advance to substep 3c with a NO outstanding.

substep.title: Write Section to Ticket Body
substep.action:
Append the drafted section to the ticket file body using the Edit tool. The section appends AFTER all prior content (which includes prior-phase summaries and any sections already written in this iteration).
If the Writing Plan uses Parts, the first section of each Part is preceded by a "# Part N: {{part_title}}" level-1 heading before its level-2 section heading.
After writing, log to session: {{written_sections}}.append(current_section).

check.if: current_section ends a checkpoint per writing_plan.review_checkpoints
check.action: Stamp last_updated on the ticket file. Use: uv run {base_dir}/scripts/stamp-frontmatter.py --file "{{ticket_path}}" --note "ticket-write checkpoint after section {{current_section.name}}; {{count}} of {{total}} sections complete."
Capture the adjustment. Apply via Edit. Re-run the section quality checklist on the adjusted content. Continue with the next section once the user reconfirms.

Exit step 3 cleanly. The bundle on disk reflects the sections written so far. Resumption: re-invoke ticket-write; its step 1 will re-load the bundle, and the for-each iterator should skip already-written sections (compare section names in the ticket body to the writing_order list).
Proceed to next iteration of step 3 (next section in writing_order).

section.title: Iteration is the engine of this step
section.description: Step 3 is a for-each over the writing_order list from the Writing Plan. The workflow engine processes each iteration with substeps 3a-3d. Within a single invocation of step 3, all sections get drafted (modulo checkpoint pauses where the user may pause).

section.title: Resumption after pause
section.description: If the user pauses at a checkpoint, the skill exits. Re-invoking ticket-write loads the bundle and starts at step 1 again. Step 3's for-each iterator needs to skip sections that are already in the ticket body — implement this by comparing section names (from writing_order) to level-2 headings in the existing ticket body. Skip any section whose heading already exists; start from the first not-yet-written section.

section.title: Length-checkpoint discipline
section.description: Per the Writing Plan, mandatory checkpoints fire at Part boundaries and before Acceptance Criteria. Optional checkpoints fire mid-Part for Parts >400 lines. The skill HONORS the plan — does not add or skip checkpoints unilaterally. If a checkpoint feels excessive at run-time, that's a verify finding for review, not a write-time decision.

section.title: The Acceptance Criteria section is special
section.description: AC is the contract. Per the Writing Plan, AC is checkpointed BEFORE it's drafted (so the user approves the foundation first) — meaning the checkpoint fires AFTER the section immediately preceding AC. Then AC is drafted; then another checkpoint fires after AC for explicit AC review. This is the single most heavily-reviewed section.
